content_en:
Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 261

The Mention Of The One Who Will Be The First To Be Dressed At That Time

‘Aaishah % reported that the Messenger of Allah #8 said, “People will be raised on the Day of Resurrection, barefooted, naked, and uncircumcised.” ‘Aaishah 4 said, “O Messenger of Allah, and what about the private parts?” He #8 said:

A BR eee eae, oe td GS 6 oy ae gl BLP “Everyman, that Day, will have enough to make him careless of others," (Ahmad). Through another chain, this hadith is also related in Bukhaaree and Muslim.

The First To Be Clothed On The Day Of Resurrection Will Be Tbraaheem, Al-Khaleel

“As We began the first creation, We shall repeat it, (it is) a promise

binding upon Us. Truly, We shall do it.” “Lo! From the creation, the first to be clothed on the Day of Resurrection will be Ibraaheem %&. And people from my nation will be brought, and they will be taken on the side of the left (ic. toward the Hellfire). Then I will say, ‘My companions,’ and it will be said to me, You do not know the sins they perpetrated after you.” Then I will say as the righteous slave said:

£4 2% pede OO Apt is

262 Book of The End : Great Trials and Tribulations

In the narration of Al-Baihagee, Ibn ‘Abbaas .& related that the Prophet #% said, “When you will be gathered, you will be naked and barefoot.” His wife said, “Will we be looking at each other?” He & said, “O so-and-so, ‘Every man, that Day, will have enough to make him careless of others.” (Al-Buhaigee)

Abu Hurairah 2 said, “The people will be raised (or gathered), and they will be barefooted, naked, and uncircumcised. They will be standing for forty years, and their gazes will be fixed on the sky. Allah will Aljamahum (bridle them) with their sweat because of the severity of their distress. Then it will be said, ‘Clothe Ibraaheem,’ and he will be clothed in two Qibiiyyahs (a Qibtiyyah is a kind of garment) from the Qibtiyyahs of Paradise. Then He will call for Muhaminad #&, and his Basin will gush forth for him: it is (as large as) the distance between Ailaa (a small sea town that is at the borderlands of Hijaaz and Sham) and Makkah. He will drink and wash. The necks of the creation will be Tagatta’at (the literal meaning of this word is ‘cut off’) from the severity of their thirst. The Messenger of Allah # said, ‘Then J will be dressed from the garments of Paradise. I will stand from or on the right of Al-Kursee (literally, the Footstool). No one from the creation will stand at that station on the Day of Judgment other than me. It will be said, ‘Ask and you will be given; intercede and your intercession will be

granted.’” A man stood up and said, “Do you desire anything for

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 263

your parents?” He #4 said, “I will intercede for them, regardless of whether | am given or prevented, and I do not hope for anything for them.” (Related by Al-Baihagee) Al-Baihagee said, “Perhaps this is before revelation was sent down to prohibit asking forgiveness for the disbelievers and praying over the hypocrites.’”

“Alee # said, “The first to be clothed will be Al-Khaleel: in two Qibtiyyahs (a kind of garment), and then Muhammad 3 in a Hullah (a kind of garment) from the right of the Throne.” (Related by AlQurtubee) ~’Abdullah ibn Mas’ood 2 reported that the Messenger of Allah 2 said, “The first to be clothed is Ibraaheem. Allah will say, ‘Clothe my Khaleel (one who is chosen for extra love; a very high ranking). Two white Reetahs (a soft, thin garment) will be brought, and he will wear them. He will then sit down, facing the Throne. Then my garment will be brought, and I will wear it. I will stand on his right, ina station that none will stand upon other than me — the first ones and the last ones will envy me for it.” Abu ‘Abdullah Al-Quradhee related this narration in Kitaab At-Tadhkirah. Jaabir # said, “The callers to prayer and the Mulabbeen (those who were making the Talbiyyah for the pilgrimage) will come out on the Day of Resurrection, with the callers te prayer making the Aadhaan and the Mulabee (singular of Mulabbeen) making the Talbiyyah. And the first to be dressed from the garments of Paradise is Ibraaheem 3A); then Muhammad #%; then the Prophets #; and then the callers to prayer.” (Related by Al-Qurtubee) Al-Qurtubee went on to mention the appropriateness of Ibraaheem 420) being given precedence in this issue. He said, “He was the first to wear Saraaweel (a kind of pants), showing extra carefulness in covering himself. Perhaps another reason is that he was stripped of his clothing on the day that he was thrown into the fire (so because of that, it is appropriate that he be dressed first on the Day of Resurrection, as a reward and honor). And Allah # knows best.”

264 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

Its chain is good, but it is neither in Al-Musnad nor in the Books.

Umm Salamah ¥ related that she heard the Prophet # say, “People will be raised (or gathered), and they will be barefooted, naked, and uncircumcised, just as they began.” Umm Salamah %& said, “O Messenger of Allah, we will be looking at one another?’ He 2 said, “People will be occupied away from that.” She asked, “And what will be their preoccupation?” He #8 said, “The spreading out of the scrolls (of deeds); in them are atom weights (of deeds) and mustard seed weights.” (Related by Abu Bakr ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa)

ok SE 3 oe gt BD

“Boery man, that Day, will have enough to make him careless of

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 265

the sky for 40 years. Sweat will reach the feet of some of them; it will reach the ankles of others; it will reach the stomachs of yet others; and some among them will be bridled by sweat from standing so long. Then, after that, Allah will have mercy on His slaves. He will order the Mugarrabeen (chosen and close ones) Angels, and they will carry His Throne from the heavens to the earth, until His Throne is placed on a white earth — upon which no blood was spilled and upon which no wrongdoing was perpetrated. It is like white silver. Then the angels will stand, surrounding the Throne. That is the first day that an eye will see Allah, He 8 will order a caller, who will call out with a voice that is heard by Afh-Thagalaan, jinn and mankind, ‘where is so-and-so, so-and-so, son of so-and-so, son of so-and-so?” Peopl> will stretch out their necks for that voice, and the one who is called will come out from the Mawgif (station, place of gathering), and A lah will make the people to be acquainted with him. Then it will be said, ‘Let his good deeds come out with him, and Allah will make the people of the Maugif to be acquainted with those good deeds. When he will stand before the Lord of all that exists, it will be said, ‘Where are the people of Al-Mazaalim?’... It will be said to each one of them, ‘Did you wrong so and so for such and such?’ He (each one) will say, ‘Yes, O my Lord.’ And that is the day wherein their tongues, hands, and legs bear witness upon them, testifying to what they used to do. The good deeds of the transgressor will be taken and then given to the one he wronged. Then there will be neither dinar nor dirham, but rather only taking of good deeds and returning (to someone else) bad deeds. The people of Madhaalim (who were wronged) will continue to take from the good deeds of the transgressor until there remains no good deed with him; then those who remain, who did not take anything, will say, ‘Why are those other than us taking their full right when we are prevented?’ It will be said to them, ‘Do not rush.’ Then their bad deeds are taken and are returned to him, until their remains no one whom he wronged with a single act of transgression (against him). Allah will make all of the people of the Mawgif (place of the gathering) to be acquainted with that. When the accountability of the wrongdoer is finished, it will be said, ‘return to your mother, Al-Haawiyah (the Hellfire), for there is no wrongdoing today, and Allah is quick to take account.’ Because of the severe accountability that they see, every angel, sent Prophet, Siddeeq (truthful One), and. Martyr will think that he will not be saved — except he whom Allah #8 protects
content_id:
Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah-Fitnah Besar dan Malapetaka 261

Penyebutan Tentang Orang Yang Pertama Kali Akan Diberi Pakaian Pada Saat Itu

'Aaishah % meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah #8 bersabda, "Manusia akan dibangkitkan pada Hari Kebangkitan dalam keadaan tidak beralas kaki, telanjang, dan tidak dikhitan." 'Aaishah % berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah, lalu bagaimana dengan aurat mereka?" Beliau #8 bersabda:

A BR eee eae, oe td GS 6 oy ae gl BLP "Setiap orang, pada Hari itu, akan memiliki kesibukan yang membuatnya tidak peduli dengan orang lain," (Ahmad). Melalui jalur lain, hadith ini juga diriwayatkan dalam Bukhaaree dan Muslim.

Orang Pertama Yang Akan Diberi Pakaian Pada Hari Kebangkitan Adalah Ibraaheem Al-Khaleel

"Sebagaimana Kami memulai penciptaan pertama, Kami akan mengulanginya, (itu adalah) janji

yang mengikat Kami. Sungguh, Kami pasti akan melakukannya." "Ketahuilah! Dari seluruh makhluk, yang pertama kali diberi pakaian pada Hari Kebangkitan adalah Ibraaheem %&. Lalu orang-orang dari umatku akan dibawa, dan mereka akan digiring ke sisi kiri (yakni menuju Neraka). Maka aku akan berkata, 'Mereka adalah sahabat-sahabatku,' dan akan dikatakan kepadaku, 'Engkau tidak mengetahui dosa-dosa yang mereka lakukan setelah kamu pergi.'" Kemudian aku akan berkata sebagaimana yang dikatakan oleh hamba yang saleh:

£4 2% pede OO Apt is

262 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah-Fitnah Besar dan Malapetaka

Dalam riwayat Al-Baihagee, Ibn 'Abbaas .& meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi #% bersabda, "Ketika kalian akan dikumpulkan, kalian akan telanjang dan tidak beralas kaki." Istrinya bertanya, "Apakah kita akan saling melihat satu sama lain?" Beliau & bersabda, "Wahai fulanah, 'Setiap orang, pada Hari itu, akan memiliki kesibukan yang membuatnya tidak peduli dengan orang lain.'" (Al-Baihagee)

Abu Hurairah % berkata, "Manusia akan dibangkitkan (atau dikumpulkan), dan mereka akan tidak beralas kaki, telanjang, dan tidak dikhitan. Mereka akan berdiri selama empat puluh tahun, dan pandangan mereka tertuju ke langit. Allah akan Aljamahum (mengekang mereka) dengan keringat mereka sendiri karena beratnya kesusahan yang mereka alami. Kemudian akan dikatakan, 'Berikan pakaian kepada Ibraaheem,' dan dia akan diberi pakaian berupa dua Qibtiyyah (sejenis pakaian) dari Qibtiyyah-Qibtiyyah surga. Kemudian Dia akan memanggil Muhammad #&, dan Haudh-nya akan memancar untuknya: luasnya (sebesar) jarak antara Ailaa (sebuah kota kecil di tepi laut yang berada di perbatasan Hijaz dan Syam) dan Makkah. Beliau akan minum dan mencuci diri. Tenggorokan-tenggorokan para makhluk akan Tagatta'at (makna harfiah kata ini adalah 'terputus') karena dahaga yang amat sangat. Rasulullah # bersabda, 'Kemudian aku akan diberi pakaian dari pakaian-pakaian surga. Aku akan berdiri di sebelah kanan Al-Kursee (secara harfiah, Pijakan Kaki). Tidak ada seorang pun dari makhluk yang akan berdiri di kedudukan itu pada Hari Kiamat selain aku. Akan dikatakan, 'Mintalah dan kamu akan diberi; berilah syafaat dan syafaatmu akan

dikabulkan.'" Seorang laki-laki berdiri dan berkata, "Apakah engkau menginginkan sesuatu untuk

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah-Fitnah Besar dan Malapetaka 263

kedua orang tuamu?" Beliau #4 bersabda, "Aku akan memberikan syafaat untuk mereka, terlepas dari apakah aku diberi atau dicegah, dan aku tidak mengharapkan apa pun untuk mereka." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Baihagee) Al-Baihagee berkata, "Barangkali ini terjadi sebelum wahyu diturunkan yang melarang meminta ampunan untuk orang-orang kafir dan menshalatkan orang-orang munafik.'"

'Alee % berkata, "Yang pertama kali diberi pakaian adalah Al-Khaleel: dalam dua Qibtiyyah (sejenis pakaian), kemudian Muhammad % dalam sebuah Hullah (sejenis pakaian) dari sebelah kanan Arsy." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Qurtubee) 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ood % meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah % bersabda, "Yang pertama kali diberi pakaian adalah Ibraaheem. Allah akan berfirman, 'Berikan pakaian kepada Khaleel-Ku (orang yang dipilih untuk mendapatkan cinta yang istimewa; kedudukan yang sangat tinggi). Dua Reetah putih (pakaian yang lembut dan tipis) akan dibawa, dan dia akan memakainya. Kemudian dia akan duduk menghadap Arsy. Lalu pakaianku akan dibawa, dan aku akan memakainya. Aku akan berdiri di sebelah kanannya, di sebuah kedudukan yang tidak akan ditempati oleh siapa pun selain aku — orang-orang terdahulu dan orang-orang kemudian akan merasa iri dengan kedudukanku itu." Abu 'Abdullah Al-Quradhee meriwayatkan narasi ini dalam Kitaab At-Tadhkirah. Jaabir % berkata, "Para muadzin dan Mulabbeen (orang-orang yang mengucapkan Talbiyyah untuk haji) akan keluar pada Hari Kebangkitan, dengan para muadzin mengumandangkan Aadhaan dan Mulabee (bentuk tunggal dari Mulabbeen) mengucapkan Talbiyyah. Dan yang pertama kali diberi pakaian dari pakaian-pakaian surga adalah Ibraaheem %A); kemudian Muhammad #%; kemudian para Nabi #%; dan kemudian para muadzin." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Qurtubee) Al-Qurtubee kemudian menyebutkan kelayakan Ibraaheem %A) untuk didahulukan dalam masalah ini. Beliau berkata, "Dia adalah orang pertama yang mengenakan Saraaweel (sejenis celana), menunjukkan kehati-hatian yang lebih dalam menutup auratnya. Mungkin alasan lainnya adalah bahwa dia pernah dilucuti pakaiannya pada hari ketika dia dilemparkan ke dalam api (sehingga karena hal itu, adalah tepat bahwa dia diberi pakaian pertama kali pada Hari Kebangkitan, sebagai pahala dan kehormatan). Dan Allah % lebih mengetahui."

264 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah-Fitnah Besar dan Malapetaka

Sanadnya baik, namun tidak terdapat dalam Al-Musnad maupun dalam kitab-kitab hadith.

Umm Salamah % meriwayatkan bahwa dia mendengar Nabi # bersabda, "Manusia akan dibangkitkan (atau dikumpulkan), dan mereka akan tidak beralas kaki, telanjang, dan tidak dikhitan, sebagaimana mereka pertama kali diciptakan." Umm Salamah %& berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah, apakah kita akan saling melihat satu sama lain?" Beliau % bersabda, "Manusia akan disibukkan dari hal itu." Dia bertanya, "Dan apakah yang akan menyibukkan mereka?" Beliau #8 bersabda, "Penyebaran lembaran-lembaran (catatan amal); di dalamnya terdapat amal seberat atom dan seberat biji sawi." (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Bakr ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa)

ok SE 3 oe gt BD

"Setiap orang, pada Hari itu, akan memiliki kesibukan yang membuatnya tidak peduli dengan

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah-Fitnah Besar dan Malapetaka 265

langit selama 40 tahun. Keringat akan mencapai kaki sebagian dari mereka; akan mencapai pergelangan kaki sebagian yang lain; akan mencapai perut sebagian yang lain lagi; dan sebagian di antara mereka akan dikekang oleh keringat karena lamanya mereka berdiri. Kemudian setelah itu, Allah akan merahmati para hamba-Nya. Dia akan memerintahkan para Malaikat Mugarrabeen (yang terpilih dan dekat dengan Allah), dan mereka akan membawa Arsy-Nya dari langit ke bumi, hingga Arsy-Nya diletakkan di atas bumi yang putih — di mana tidak pernah ada darah yang tertumpah dan di mana tidak pernah ada kezaliman yang dilakukan. Bumi itu bagaikan perak putih. Kemudian para malaikat akan berdiri mengelilingi Arsy. Itulah hari pertama di mana sebuah mata akan melihat Allah, Dia #8 akan memerintahkan seorang pemanggil, yang akan memanggil dengan suara yang terdengar oleh Ats-Tsagalaan, jin dan manusia, 'Di manakah si fulan, fulan, putra fulan, putra fulan?' Manusia akan mengulurkan leher mereka untuk suara itu, dan orang yang dipanggil akan keluar dari Mawqif (tempat berdiri, tempat pengumpulan), dan Allah akan memperkenalkannya kepada seluruh manusia. Kemudian akan dikatakan, 'Biarkan amal-amal baiknya keluar bersamanya,' dan Allah akan memperkenalkan amal-amal baik tersebut kepada para penghuni Mawqif. Ketika dia berdiri di hadapan Tuhan semesta alam, akan dikatakan, 'Di manakah orang-orang yang pernah dizalimi?'... Akan dikatakan kepada masing-masing dari mereka, 'Apakah kamu pernah menzalimi si fulan dengan perbuatan ini dan itu?' Dia (masing-masing) akan berkata, 'Ya, wahai Tuhanku.' Dan itulah hari di mana lisan, tangan, dan kaki mereka menjadi saksi atas diri mereka, bersaksi tentang apa yang mereka lakukan. Amal-amal baik orang yang berbuat zalim akan diambil kemudian diberikan kepada orang yang dia zalimi. Maka tidak ada lagi dinar maupun dirham, melainkan hanya pengambilan amal-amal baik dan pengembalian (kepada orang lain) amal-amal buruk. Para penghuni Madzaalim (mereka yang pernah dizalimi) akan terus mengambil dari amal-amal baik orang yang berbuat zalim hingga tidak tersisa satu pun amal baik padanya; kemudian mereka yang tersisa, yang belum mengambil apa pun, akan berkata, 'Mengapa mereka yang lain mengambil hak mereka sepenuhnya sementara kami dicegah?' Akan dikatakan kepada mereka, 'Janganlah tergesa-gesa.' Kemudian amal-amal buruk mereka diambil dan dikembalikan kepadanya, hingga tidak tersisa seorang pun yang pernah dia zalimi dengan satu pun perbuatan zalim (terhadapnya). Allah akan memperkenalkan semua itu kepada seluruh penghuni Mawqif (tempat pengumpulan). Ketika hisab orang yang berbuat zalim telah selesai, akan dikatakan, 'Kembalilah ke indukmu, Al-Haawiyah (Neraka), karena tidak ada kezaliman hari ini, dan Allah Maha Cepat dalam perhitungan.' Karena beratnya hisab yang mereka saksikan, setiap malaikat, Nabi yang diutus, Siddeeq (orang yang sangat jujur), dan Syahid akan mengira bahwa dirinya tidak akan selamat — kecuali orang yang Allah #8 lindungi
content_ar: الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 261 The Mention Of The One Who Will Be The أولاً To Be Dressed At That Time ‘Aaishah % reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله #8 said, “People will be raised on the Day of البعث, barefooted, naked, and uncircumcised.” ‘Aaishah 4 said, “O Messenger of الله, and what about the private parts?” He #8 said: A BR eee eae, oe td GS 6 oy ae gl BLP “Everyman, that Day, will have enough to make him careless of others," (أحمد). Through another chain, this الحديث is أيضاً related in Bukhaaree and مسلم. The أولاً To Be Clothed On The Day Of البعث Will Be Tbraaheem, Al-Khaleel “As We began the أولاً creation, We shall repeat it, (it is) a promise binding upon Us. Truly, We shall do it.” “Lo! From the creation, the أولاً to be clothed on the Day of البعث will be Ibraaheem %&. And people from my nation will be brought, and they will be taken on the side of the left (ic. toward the النار). Then I will say, ‘My الصحابة,’ and it will be said to me, You do not know the الذنوب they perpetrated بعد you.” Then I will say as the الصالح slave said: £4 2% pede OO Apt is 262 الكتاب of The End : Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations In the narration of Al-Baihagee, Ibn ‘Abbaas .& related that النبي ﷺ #% said, “عندما you will be gathered, you will be naked and barefoot.” His الزوجة said, “Will we be looking at بعضهم بعضاً?” He & said, “O لذا-and-لذا, ‘Every man, that Day, will have enough to make him careless of others.” (Al-Buhaigee) أبو هريرة 2 said, “The people will be raised (or gathered), and they will be barefooted, naked, and uncircumcised. They will be standing for forty years, and their gazes will be fixed on the sky. الله will Aljamahum (bridle them) with their sweat لأن of the severity of their distress. Then it will be said, ‘Clothe Ibraaheem,’ and he will be clothed in two Qibiiyyahs (a Qibtiyyah is a kind of garment) from the Qibtiyyahs of الجنة. Then He will call for Muhaminad #&, and his Basin will gush forth for him: it is (as large as) the distance between Ailaa (a small sea town that is at the borderlands of Hijaaz and Sham) and مكة المكرمة. He will drink and wash. The necks of the creation will be Tagatta’at (the literal meaning of this word is ‘cut off’) from the severity of their thirst. الرسول ﷺ of الله # said, ‘Then J will be dressed from the garments of الجنة. I will stand from or on the الحق of Al-Kursee (literally, the Footstool). No one from the creation will stand at that station on the يوم القيامة other than me. It will be said, ‘Ask and you will be given; intercede and your intercession will be granted.’” A man stood up and said, “Do you desire anything for الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 263 your الوالدان?” He #4 said, “I will intercede for them, regardless of whether | am given or prevented, and I do not الأمل for anything for them.” (Related by Al-Baihagee) Al-Baihagee said, “Perhaps this is قبل الوحي was sent down to prohibit asking المغفرة for the disbelievers and praying over the hypocrites.’” “Alee # said, “The أولاً to be clothed will be Al-Khaleel: in two Qibtiyyahs (a kind of garment), and then محمد ﷺ 3 in a Hullah (a kind of garment) from the الحق of the Throne.” (Related by AlQurtubee) ~’Abdullah ibn Mas’ood 2 reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2 said, “The أولاً to be clothed is Ibraaheem. الله will say, ‘Clothe my Khaleel (one who is chosen for extra المحبة; a very high ranking). Two white Reetahs (a soft, thin garment) will be brought, and he will wear them. He will then sit down, facing the Throne. Then my garment will be brought, and I will wear it. I will stand on his الحق, ina station that none will stand upon other than me — the أولاً ones and the last ones will الحسد me for it.” Abu ‘Abdullah Al-Quradhee related this narration in Kitaab At-Tadhkirah. Jaabir # said, “The callers to الصلاة and the Mulabbeen (those who were making the Talbiyyah for the pilgrimage) will come out on the Day of البعث, with the callers te الصلاة making the Aadhaan and the Mulabee (singular of Mulabbeen) making the Talbiyyah. And the أولاً to be dressed from the garments of الجنة is Ibraaheem 3A); then محمد ﷺ #%; then the الأنبياء #; and then the callers to الصلاة.” (Related by Al-Qurtubee) Al-Qurtubee went on to mention the appropriateness of Ibraaheem 420) being given precedence in this issue. قال, “He was the أولاً to wear Saraaweel (a kind of pants), showing extra carefulness in covering himself. Perhaps another العقل is that he was stripped of his clothing on the day that he was thrown into the fire (لذا لأن of that, it is appropriate that he be dressed أولاً on the Day of البعث, as a الأجر and الشرف). And الله # knows best.” 264 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations Its chain is الخير, but it is neither in Al-Musnad nor in the الكتب. Umm Salamah ¥ related that she heard النبي ﷺ # say, “People will be raised (or gathered), and they will be barefooted, naked, and uncircumcised, just as they began.” Umm Salamah %& said, “O Messenger of الله, we will be looking at بعضهم بعضاً?’ He 2 said, “People will be occupied away from that.” She asked, “And what will be their preoccupation?” He #8 said, “The spreading out of the scrolls (of deeds); in them are atom weights (of deeds) and mustard seed weights.” (Related by أبو بكر ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa) ok SE 3 oe gt BD “Boery man, that Day, will have enough to make him careless of الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 265 the sky for 40 years. Sweat will reach the feet of some of them; it will reach the ankles of others; it will reach the stomachs of yet others; and some among them will be bridled by sweat from standing لذا long. Then, بعد that, الله will have الرحمة on His slaves. He will order the Mugarrabeen (chosen and close ones) الملائكة, and they will carry His Throne from the heavens to the earth, حتى His Throne is placed on a white earth — upon which no blood was spilled and upon which no الإساءة was perpetrated. It is like white silver. Then the الملائكة will stand, surrounding the Throne. That is the أولاً day that an eye will see الله, He 8 will order a caller, who will call out with a voice that is heard by Afh-Thagalaan, الجن and mankind, ‘where is لذا-and-لذا, لذا-and-لذا, son of لذا-and-لذا, son of لذا-and-لذا?” Peopl> will stretch out their necks for that voice, and the one who is called will come out from the Mawgif (station, place of gathering), and A lah will make the people to be acquainted with him. Then it will be said, ‘Let his الخير deeds come out with him, and الله will make the people of the Maugif to be acquainted with those الخير deeds. عندما he will stand قبل the Lord of all that exists, it will be said, ‘Where are the people of Al-Mazaalim?’... It will be said to each one of them, ‘Did you الخطأ لذا and لذا for such and such?’ He (each one) will say, ‘Yes, O my Lord.’ And that is the day wherein their tongues, hands, and legs bear witness upon them, testifying to what they used to do. The الخير deeds of the transgressor will be taken and then given to the one he wronged. Then there will be neither dinar nor dirham, but rather only taking of الخير deeds and returning (to someone else) bad deeds. The people of Madhaalim (who were wronged) will continue to take from the الخير deeds of the transgressor حتى there remains no الخير deed with him; then those who remain, who did not take anything, will say, ‘Why are those other than us taking their full الحق عندما we are prevented?’ It will be said to them, ‘Do not rush.’ Then their bad deeds are taken and are returned to him, حتى their remains no one whom he wronged with a single act of transgression (against him). الله will make all of the people of the Mawgif (place of the gathering) to be acquainted with that. عندما the accountability of the wrongdoer is finished, it will be said, ‘return to your الأم, Al-Haawiyah (the النار), for there is no الإساءة today, and الله is quick to take account.’ لأن of the severe accountability that they see, every angel, sent النبي, Siddeeq (truthful One), and. Martyr will think that he will not be saved — except he whom الله #8 protects
